title: Azure Functions Capabilities
content: Azure Functions is a serverless compute service. Supports HTTP, queue, timer, blob, and event triggers. Consumption plan scales to zero and charges per execution. Cold start latency is 1-3 seconds. Max execution time is 10 minutes on Consumption plan. 99.95% SLA. Best for event-driven, sporadic, or burst workloads.

title: Azure Functions Pricing
content: Consumption plan: $0.20 per million executions, $0.000016 per GB-second of memory. First 400,000 GB-seconds and 1 million executions free per month. Premium plan eliminates cold starts and costs ~$140/month minimum. Best for sporadic workloads where pay-per-use saves money over always-on App Service.

title: Azure App Service Capabilities
content: Azure App Service is a fully managed platform for hosting web applications and REST APIs. Supports .NET, Node.js, Python, Java, PHP. Basic tier gives 1 core, 1.75 GB RAM. Standard tier adds auto-scaling and custom domains. No cold starts. 99.95% SLA. Best for always-on web APIs and web apps with predictable traffic.

title: Azure App Service Pricing
content: Basic B1: ~$13/month, 1 core, 1.75 GB RAM. Standard S1: ~$70/month, 1 core, 1.75 GB RAM with auto-scale. Premium P1v3: ~$140/month, 2 cores, 8 GB RAM. Best for sustained workloads where serverless cold starts are unacceptable.

title: Azure Container Apps Capabilities
content: Azure Container Apps is a serverless container hosting service built on Kubernetes. Supports KEDA-based auto-scaling including scale-to-zero. No Kubernetes management required. Supports Dapr for microservice patterns. 99.95% SLA. Best for containerized microservices and background jobs that need Kubernetes capabilities without the overhead.

title: Azure Kubernetes Service Capabilities
content: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes cluster. Full control over pods, deployments, and cluster configuration. Supports any container workload. Node pools for different VM sizes. Integrated with Azure Monitor, AD, and networking. Best for complex microservices requiring full Kubernetes control. Higher operational overhead than Container Apps.

title: Azure Kubernetes Service Pricing
content: AKS control plane is free. You pay for VM nodes: Standard DS2 v2 (~$140/month for 3 nodes). Total cluster cost typically $200-500/month for a basic production setup. Much more expensive than Container Apps or Functions for simple workloads. Consider Container Apps first unless you need full Kubernetes control.

title: Azure Blob Storage Capabilities
content: Azure Blob Storage is object storage for unstructured data. Hot tier for frequently accessed data, Cool tier for infrequent access (30-day min), Archive tier for rarely accessed data (180-day min). 99.9% to 99.99% availability SLA depending on redundancy. Supports up to 5 PB per storage account. Integrates with Event Grid for upload triggers.

title: Azure Blob Storage Pricing
content: Hot tier: $0.018/GB/month for first 50 TB. Cool tier: $0.01/GB/month (40% cheaper, higher access costs). Archive tier: $0.00099/GB/month (cheapest storage, high retrieval cost and latency). For image processing pipelines, Hot tier for input/output and Cool for processed results reduces cost significantly.

title: Azure Cosmos DB Capabilities
content: Azure Cosmos DB is a globally distributed NoSQL database. Supports SQL, MongoDB, Cassandra, Gremlin, and Table APIs. Sub-10ms latency at the 99th percentile. Multi-region writes for global availability. Serverless mode for sporadic workloads. Automatic indexing. 99.999% SLA with multi-region. Best for globally distributed apps needing low latency.

title: Azure Cosmos DB Pricing
content: Provisioned throughput: $0.008 per RU/s per hour (~$6/month per 100 RU/s). Serverless: $0.25 per million RUs consumed. Storage: $0.25/GB/month. Multi-region write multiplies cost by number of regions. Serverless mode is cheapest for low or unpredictable traffic. Provisioned with autoscale is best for production.

title: Azure SQL Database Capabilities
content: Azure SQL Database is a fully managed relational database. Compatible with SQL Server. Supports up to 4TB storage. Automatic backups, patching, and high availability. DTU-based and vCore-based purchasing models. Built-in intelligence for performance optimization. Best for transactional workloads with complex queries and ACID requirements.

title: Azure SQL Database Pricing
content: Basic: ~$5/month, 5 DTUs, 2GB. Standard S0: ~$15/month, 10 DTUs. Standard S2: ~$75/month, 50 DTUs. General Purpose 2 vCores: ~$370/month. For demo and small workloads, Basic or S0 is sufficient. Upgrade to Standard S2 for production API workloads.

title: Azure Service Bus Capabilities
content: Azure Service Bus is an enterprise message broker. Supports queues (point-to-point) and topics (pub/sub). FIFO delivery, dead-letter queues, message sessions, and duplicate detection. AMQP protocol. Standard tier: 256KB max message size. Premium tier: 1MB messages, dedicated resources, no cold start, network isolation. Best for reliable, ordered, transactional messaging.

title: Azure Service Bus Pricing
content: Basic: $0.05 per million operations, queues only. Standard: $10/month base + $0.05 per million operations, includes topics. Premium: ~$678/month per messaging unit, dedicated resources. For most architectures, Standard tier at ~$10-15/month is sufficient. Use Service Bus to decouple compute from storage and eliminate single points of failure.

title: Azure Event Grid Capabilities
content: Azure Event Grid is a fully managed event routing service. Push-based delivery to webhooks, Azure Functions, Logic Apps, Service Bus. Supports custom topics and Azure service events (Blob uploads, resource changes). At-least-once delivery guarantee. Dead-letter destinations for failed events. Best for event-driven architectures where services react to changes.

title: Azure Event Grid Pricing
content: First 100,000 operations/month free. $0.60 per million operations after that. Extremely cost-effective for event routing. For an image processing pipeline at 10k images/minute, cost is approximately $0.60 * 10k * 60 * 24 * 30 / 1M = ~$26/month. Much cheaper than Service Bus for simple event triggering.

title: Azure Event Hubs Capabilities
content: Azure Event Hubs is a big data streaming platform and event ingestion service. Processes millions of events per second. Kafka-compatible API. Capture feature streams events directly to Blob Storage. Partitioned consumer model for parallel processing. Best for high-throughput telemetry, log aggregation, and IoT data ingestion. Not ideal for point-to-point messaging.

title: Azure Event Hubs Pricing
content: Standard: $0.028 per throughput unit/hour (~$11/month per TU at 1 MB/s ingress, 2 MB/s egress). Premium: $0.107 per processing unit/hour. 1 TU handles ~1MB/s or 1000 events/second. For IoT telemetry at 10k events/second, need 10 TUs = ~$110/month. Dedicated tier for very high throughput.

title: Azure Front Door Capabilities
content: Azure Front Door is a global load balancer, CDN, and WAF combined. Routes users to nearest healthy origin using anycast. Built-in DDoS protection and WAF. SSL offloading. Health probes and automatic failover. Reduces latency for global users by serving from edge PoPs. Best for global web applications needing low latency across regions.

title: Azure Front Door Pricing
content: Standard tier: ~$35/month base + data transfer and routing rule costs. Premium tier: ~$330/month base with WAF and Private Link. For global latency improvement, Front Door adds ~$35-50/month and can reduce user-facing latency from 450ms to under 150ms for globally distributed users.

title: Azure API Management Capabilities
content: Azure API Management is a full lifecycle API gateway. Features: rate limiting, authentication (OAuth, API keys, JWT), request/response transformation, caching, versioning, developer portal. Developer tier for non-production. Consumption tier is serverless and pay-per-call. Best for centralizing API security, throttling, and governance across microservices.

title: Azure API Management Pricing
content: Consumption tier: $3.50 per million API calls (pay-per-call, no base cost). Developer tier: ~$48/month (no SLA, not for production). Basic tier: ~$140/month. Standard tier: ~$700/month. For demo purposes, Developer tier at $48/month is sufficient. Consumption tier is cheapest for low-traffic APIs.

title: Azure Monitor and Application Insights Capabilities
content: Azure Monitor collects metrics, logs, and traces from Azure resources. Application Insights is an APM tool built on Azure Monitor for application-level telemetry. Features: distributed tracing, live metrics, dependency tracking, failure analysis, performance monitoring. Application Insights SDK integrates with Functions, App Service, and Container Apps automatically.

title: Azure OpenAI Service Capabilities
content: Azure OpenAI Service provides access to OpenAI models (GPT-4o, GPT-4, GPT-3.5-turbo, DALL-E, Whisper, Embeddings) through Azure infrastructure. Enterprise SLA, data privacy (prompts not used for training), private networking options. Rate limits based on deployment quota. Best for AI features requiring compliance, private data handling, and Azure integration.

title: Azure AI Search Capabilities
content: Azure AI Search (formerly Cognitive Search) is a cloud search service supporting full-text and vector search. Supports semantic ranking, skills pipeline for AI enrichment, and hybrid search combining keyword and vector. Free tier: 50MB, 10k docs, 3 indexes. Basic tier: 2GB, 1M docs. Integrates with Azure OpenAI for RAG patterns.

title: Image Processing Pipeline Pattern
content: Recommended architecture for image processing: Azure Blob Storage (Hot tier) receives uploads, Azure Event Grid triggers on new blob uploads, Azure Functions processes images using Consumption plan for cost efficiency, processed images stored in Blob Storage (Cool tier). Add Application Insights for observability. For 10k images/minute, Functions auto-scales. Total cost: ~$150-250/month.

title: Web API Architecture Pattern
content: Standard web API pattern: Azure App Service hosts the REST API, Azure SQL Database stores relational data, Azure Blob Storage handles file uploads, Azure API Management provides rate limiting and auth. Add Application Insights for monitoring. For cost optimization, replace App Service with Azure Functions on Consumption plan. Total cost: $50-200/month depending on tier.

title: RAG AI Workload Pattern
content: Retrieval-Augmented Generation pattern: Azure Blob Storage holds source documents, Azure AI Search indexes and enables vector search, Azure Functions orchestrates embedding and retrieval, Azure OpenAI Service generates grounded answers, Azure Cosmos DB stores conversation history. This pattern eliminates LLM hallucination by grounding responses in retrieved documents.

title: Streaming and IoT Pipeline Pattern
content: High-throughput streaming pattern: Azure Event Hubs ingests telemetry at millions of events/second, Azure Functions or Stream Analytics processes in real-time, Azure Cosmos DB or Azure SQL stores aggregated results, Azure Monitor tracks pipeline health. For IoT at 100k events/second, use Event Hubs with 100 throughput units. Add dead-letter processing for failed events.

title: Microservices Architecture Pattern
content: Microservices pattern on Azure: Azure Kubernetes Service or Container Apps hosts services, Azure Service Bus enables async inter-service communication, Azure Cosmos DB provides per-service data stores, Azure Front Door routes global traffic, Azure API Management acts as the gateway. Start with Container Apps to avoid AKS overhead. Add KEDA for event-driven scaling.

title: Single Point of Failure Mitigation
content: A single point of failure (SPOF) is any component whose failure causes complete system outage. Mitigation strategies: place Azure Service Bus or Event Grid between producers and consumers to buffer load, use zone-redundant storage and databases, deploy compute to multiple regions with Front Door routing, enable auto-scaling so one instance failure triggers replacement. Azure Service Bus Premium provides geo-disaster recovery.

title: Cost Optimization Patterns
content: Azure cost optimization strategies: replace App Service with Azure Functions Consumption plan for sporadic workloads (85% cost reduction), downgrade SQL Database from Standard to Basic for dev/test, switch Blob Storage from Hot to Cool tier for infrequently accessed data (40% savings), use Cosmos DB Serverless for low-traffic databases, use Container Apps instead of AKS (60% cheaper for most workloads).

title: High Availability and Disaster Recovery
content: Azure HA patterns: deploy to paired regions (East US + West US 2), use Azure Front Door for automatic failover, enable geo-replication on Cosmos DB and SQL Database, use zone-redundant storage (ZRS), deploy App Service or Container Apps in multiple regions, use Azure Service Bus Premium for geo-disaster recovery on messaging. Target 99.99% availability requires multi-region active-active.

title: Security and Zero Trust Pattern
content: Azure security patterns: Azure API Management centralizes authentication (OAuth 2.0, JWT validation, API keys), Azure Key Vault stores secrets and certificates, Managed Identity eliminates credential management, Private Endpoints keep traffic off public internet, Azure DDoS Protection guards against volumetric attacks, WAF on Front Door blocks OWASP top 10. Zero trust: verify explicitly, use least privilege, assume breach.

title: Azure AI Search Pricing
content: Azure AI Search pricing is based on search units (SUs = replicas x partitions). Free tier: 1 SU, 50MB, 10k docs, 3 indexes — $0/month. Basic tier: $0.101/SU/hour = $73.73/SU/month, max 3 replicas x 1 partition. Standard S1: $0.336/SU/hour = $245/SU/month, max 12 replicas x 12 partitions. Standard S2: $1.008/SU/hour = $735/SU/month. Standard S3: $2.016/SU/hour = $1,471/SU/month. Storage Optimized L1: $2.688/SU/hour = $1,962/SU/month. Semantic ranking: included free on Basic+, was previously charged separately. For a single-replica RAG demo: Basic = $73.73/month. For production with 2 replicas (HA): Basic = $147.46/month, S1 = $490/month. For high query load (10k+ req/min): S1 with 3 replicas = $735/month. Rule of thumb: add 1 replica per 36 QPS for Basic, 60 QPS for S1.

title: Azure OpenAI Service Pricing
content: Azure OpenAI pricing is per 1,000 tokens (input + output billed separately). GPT-4o: $0.005/1K input tokens, $0.015/1K output tokens. GPT-4o mini: $0.000165/1K input, $0.00066/1K output. GPT-4 Turbo: $0.030/1K input, $0.060/1K output. GPT-3.5 Turbo: $0.0005/1K input, $0.0015/1K output. o3-mini: $0.0011/1K input, $0.0044/1K output. Typical RAG request: 1,000 input tokens + 500 output tokens. At 500 req/min (low scale): GPT-4o costs ~$108/month input + $162/month output = $270/month. At same scale GPT-3.5 Turbo costs ~$270/month / 10 = $27/month. Provisioned throughput (PTU) available for high-volume predictable workloads at lower per-token cost.

title: Azure Cosmos DB Pricing Detail
content: Cosmos DB billing modes: Provisioned throughput at $0.008 per 100 RU/s per hour (minimum 400 RU/s = $23.36/month). Autoscale provisioned at $0.012 per 100 RU/s per hour for max RUs. Serverless at $0.25 per million RUs consumed — best for dev/test and low unpredictable traffic. Storage: $0.25/GB/month. Multi-region write multiplies throughput cost by number of regions. At 500 req/min with 10 RU/request: need 83 RU/s → round up to 400 RU/s minimum → $23.36/month provisioned. At 5,000 req/min: 833 RU/s → $40/month. At 50,000 req/min: 8,333 RU/s → $400/month.
